verses.text: The beams of your courts in waters you laid; On wings of the wind your pathway you made. The clouds are your chariot; the winds do your will; The flames and the lightnings your pleasure fulfill.
You set earth’s foundations firm and secure, that earth may, unshaken, ever endure. The deep like a garment about it you cast; The waters stood high; over mountains they passed.
You water the hills with rain from your sky, With fruit of your works the earth is supplied. To nourish the cattle you make grass to grow; For man’s daily labor, the plants you bestow.

So man brings forth food by working the ground, And wine makes his heart with gladness abound; To make his face shine he extracts fragrant oil, Makes bread that will strengthen his heart for his toil.
How many your works which fill all the world, All creatures you made in wisdom, O Lord, Consider the ocean, how great and how wide, Where small and great creatures unnumbered abide!
O may the Lord’s glory evermore strand! The Lord shall enjoy the works of his hand. He looks on the earth and it trembles in fear; He touches the mountains, the smoke then appears.
As long as I live, the LORD I will praise; To God I will sing through all of my days. May my meditation be pleasing to him; my heart in the LORD then with gladness will brim.
Let wicked ones be consumed from the earth, may sinners exist no longer henceforth. My soul, bless the LORD! May his name be adored! O sing hallelujah! Give praise to the LORD!
